label: BREAKING
hook: TRUMP DOUBLES TARIFFS ON CHINA TO 60% EFFECTIVE NOW
stat1: US imports ~$370B in Chinese goods yearly
stat3: China promised immediate retaliation; markets sold off
tts_text: Breaking. President Trump signs an executive order doubling tariffs on Chinese imports to sixty percent, effective immediately. Electronics, electric vehicles, and semiconductors are the primary targets.
caption:
Trump just signed an executive order doubling tariffs on Chinese imports to 60 percent, targeting electronics, EVs, semiconductors, and steel. Beijing pledged immediate retaliation. Markets sold off pre-bell.

Here's the real arithmetic: The US imports roughly 370 billion dollars in Chinese goods annually. A 60 percent tariff doesn't fall on Beijing—it falls on American importers and, eventually, consumers at checkout. Domestic prices on laptops, phones, solar panels, and car batteries will rise. The Treasury collects the duty, not China.

That said, this isn't economic theater. Trump is betting that supply-chain reshoring and retaliatory pain on China's export sector outweigh the domestic cost shock. Beijing will likely target US agricultural exports and services. The real test: whether this breaks the dollar's role in trade settlement or just triggers a painful recalibration of manufacturing geography.

— The Clam Letter
